gaps: No existing test case covers DFU (Device Firmware Update) functionality for the Charge Case.
No test case verifies the Update button state after a successful DFU.
No test case checks for stale build number persistence causing immediate DFU failure.
No test case validates that the UI refreshes correctly after a DFU without requiring an app restart.
The bug scenario involves post-DFU UI state management, which is entirely untested.

generated_testcases.title: TC_DFU_001: Verify Update button state after successful Charge Case DFU
steps: 1. Launch the app and connect to the Charge Case.
2. Navigate to the Device Firmware Update (DFU) screen for the Charge Case.
3. Note the current firmware version and build number displayed.
4. Initiate a DFU by tapping the 'Update' button.
5. Wait for the DFU process to complete successfully (progress reaches 100% and success indicator appears).
6. Observe the firmware version displayed on the DFU screen.
7. Check the state (enabled/disabled) of the 'Update' button.
8. Compare the displayed build number with the build number noted before the update.
generated_testcases.expected: After successful DFU, the firmware version should reflect the new version. The 'Update' button should be disabled (greyed out) or show 'Up-to-date' status. The build number should be updated to the new build number, not the previous one.

generated_testcases.title: TC_DFU_002: Verify no stale build number persistence causing immediate DFU failure
steps: 1. Launch the app and connect to the Charge Case.
2. Navigate to the DFU screen and note the current firmware version and build number.
3. Perform a successful DFU update.
4. After the update completes, observe the displayed firmware version and build number.
5. Without restarting the app, tap the 'Update' button again (if still active).
6. Observe the system behavior when attempting a second DFU.
generated_testcases.expected: After a successful DFU, the 'Update' button should be disabled. If the button is still active, tapping it should NOT immediately fail with an error. The build number should be updated to the new version, preventing the system from attempting to re-flash the same firmware.

generated_testcases.title: TC_DFU_003: Verify UI refreshes correctly after DFU without requiring app restart
steps: 1. Launch the app and connect to the Charge Case.
2. Navigate to the DFU screen and record the current firmware version and build number.
3. Perform a successful DFU update.
4. After the update completes, do NOT restart the app.
5. Navigate away from the DFU screen (e.g., go to the main device screen or settings).
6. Navigate back to the DFU screen.
7. Observe the firmware version, build number, and Update button state.
8. Compare the displayed information with the information recorded before the update.
generated_testcases.expected: Without restarting the app, the DFU screen should display the updated firmware version and build number. The 'Update' button should be disabled. The UI should reflect the post-DFU state correctly without requiring an app restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC_DFU_004: Verify DFU screen state after app restart following successful Charge Case DFU
steps: 1. Launch the app and connect to the Charge Case.
2. Navigate to the DFU screen and record the current firmware version and build number.
3. Perform a successful DFU update.
4. After the update completes, force-close the app (swipe away from recent apps).
5. Re-launch the app and reconnect to the Charge Case.
6. Navigate to the DFU screen.
7. Observe the firmware version, build number, and Update button state.
generated_testcases.expected: After app restart, the DFU screen should display the updated firmware version and build number. The 'Update' button should be disabled, indicating the Charge Case is up-to-date.

generated_testcases.title: TC_DFU_005: Verify DFU failure when attempting to update with same build number (bug reproduction)
steps: 1. Launch the app and connect to the Charge Case.
2. Navigate to the DFU screen and note the current firmware version and build number.
3. Perform a successful DFU update.
4. After the update completes, observe that the 'Update' button remains active (bug scenario).
5. Tap the 'Update' button again.
6. Observe the system behavior during the second DFU attempt.
7. Note any error messages, failure indicators, or unexpected behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: This test reproduces the bug scenario. If the bug is present, the second DFU attempt will immediately fail because the system tries to flash the same build number. The expected behavior after the fix is that the 'Update' button should be disabled, preventing this scenario entirely.

generated_testcases.title: TC_DFU_006: Verify firmware version comparison logic prevents redundant updates
steps: 1. Launch the app and connect to the Charge Case.
2. Navigate to the DFU screen.
3. Note the current firmware version (e.g., v1.0.0) and build number (e.g., build 123).
4. Simulate a scenario where the server returns the same firmware version and build number as currently installed.
5. Observe the 'Update' button state and any on-screen messaging.
6. Attempt to tap the 'Update' button if it is enabled.
generated_testcases.expected: When the installed firmware version matches the available update version, the 'Update' button should be disabled. The app should display a message indicating that the Charge Case is already up-to-date. Tapping the button should not initiate a DFU process.

generated_testcases.title: TC_DFU_007: Verify DFU progress and completion indicators
steps: 1. Launch the app and connect to the Charge Case.
2. Navigate to the DFU screen.
3. Initiate a DFU update.
4. During the update, observe the progress indicator (e.g., progress bar, percentage).
5. Wait for the update to complete.
6. Observe the completion indicator (e.g., success message, checkmark, status change).
7. Verify that the progress indicator reaches 100% and the completion indicator is displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: During DFU, a progress indicator should be visible and update in real-time. Upon completion, the progress should reach 100% and a clear success indicator should be displayed (e.g., 'Update successful', green checkmark).

generated_testcases.title: TC_DFU_008: Verify DFU cancellation does not corrupt firmware state
steps: 1. Launch the app and connect to the Charge Case.
2. Navigate to the DFU screen and record the current firmware version and build number.
3. Initiate a DFU update.
4. During the update (e.g., at 30-50% progress), cancel the update using the available cancel/abort option.
5. Observe the system behavior after cancellation.
6. Navigate away from the DFU screen and back.
7. Observe the firmware version, build number, and Update button state.
8. Force-close and re-launch the app, then check the DFU screen again.
generated_testcases.expected: After cancelling a DFU, the Charge Case should remain functional with the previous firmware version. The DFU screen should display the original firmware version and build number. The 'Update' button should remain enabled, allowing the user to retry the update. No corruption or partial update state should persist.
